reviews.review_description: The game is realy good BUT is nothing like the original game and is realy short and repetitive.

Fun humor, stylish characters, and smooth, intuitive controls. The gameplay feels easy and engaging from the very first minutes. It would be even better if the chicken-computer could be hit not only with fists but with any item you happen to have in hand :)

I played first breathedge and well if you came to play something similar but vr...then no..its much much different and ...worse
Story:is barly interesting and jokes are fine just remember to save before what they call "choice"(which lead to same boss fight) because i will need now to reply whole game for that second ending
Length of game... barly 2:50 and i played slow looking around and all ... it's too short...
It's not breathedge its looking little bit...but game is linear....jokes are meh... Voice acting is ok... music is fine...combat? Boring
No reply value....i don't recommend the game ..

Game of the year.

🍧

This is a great VR experience. An absolutely successful prequel to the original game. Fun, interesting and a space chicken is what everyone needs! 
З.Ы. Русский нужен - вот прямо как воздух ...

I really like this game.  The flying, the colorful graphics, the basic gameplay, I even like the satirical plot.  
 
But the chicken.  Man, the chicken is NOT funny.  The delivery is annoying and the jokes themselves aren’t funny anyway.  Half the time they seem not to relate to anything that’s going on.  And it never, NEVER stops.  It’s just constantly chattering at you..  Can there please be a make the chicken shut up option? He needs to talk sometimes for exposition and instruction, but some way to make him quit endlessly jabbering would be much appreciated.

So glad to see the game in VR.. Played to original game a lot and seeing their next game but in VR?! Amazing! Great graphics, excellent support team and the dev’s have worked hard to make a funny and entertaining game. Looking forward to a sequel, worth every penny!

reviews.review_title: Not what I was hoping for but still good
reviews.review_description:
I was really excited for this one, being a huge fan of the crafting survival game that came out on PC and consoles. I was a little disappointed when I finished the game in one 3 hour session though and was really disappointed to find out there was no base building or survival crafting elements like the game this was based off of. For the price i feel like there shoulda been more content and I really hope they update this game with more in the future. I think it would be awesome to give us our own plot of land in the form of one of those domed farms and let us build our own farms and bases/houses. And maybe make us have to periodically protect it from waves of enemies. There’s just so much wasted potential that could’ve made this go from a good game to a great one. 

As of right now I’d have to say wait for a sale, or wait and see if they announce future content because right now it’s not worth the 30$(CDN). It’s a fun game none the less I just hope this isn’t all we get because right now there’s no replayability and this game needs that desperately. The world’s full of resources with no need for 90% of them just a handful of each. 
I’ll give it 5 stars if they announce a base building/survival update this game is in desperate need of. 

Cheers

reviews.review_title: Weirdly exciting, and I love it!
reviews.review_description:
I don't have much experience in VR games, so many of the mechanics in Breathedge, space and the feeling of weightlessness were a new and incredibly cool gaming experience for me!

In my opinion, the levels are picturesque, but not loaded with unnecessary elements. I really wanted to explore the locations and look at the small details on them.

Playing in a VR headset, I often feel seasick when moving smoothly in the game world, but in this case, the movement felt pleasant and seasickness overtook me only after 30 minutes of playing.

I am a real lazy person and prefer to play VR games with a minimum number of movements, and Breathedge completely satisfied me in this aspect, since it can be comfortably passed sitting and not moving around the room.

The training and the very first search for components were slightly delayed, but as I progress, exploring the area became easier and more interesting. Throwing some rubbish into outer space was pure joy. Humor and companion-the chicken are also on top!

Thanks to the developers for a great gaming experience!

Chicken and cow are my favorite characters in the game!

In general, it looks like a space quest with very cool control.

And you will fly like a spider, collect resources, kill robots and do quests - and all you can do is the need to recharge your headset!

Chicken jokes, awesome soundtrack and constant phone calls immerse you in the world of the game and do not let you go at all!

I feel like exploring, movement is good, grappling hook - cool.  I was a little afraid, that I will feel uncomfortable in space VR game, but flight feels surprisingly comfortable. Sometimes you have to beat the funny dumb chicken, but it's worth it :) Want to play more!

I LOVED Breathedge v1 ... waiting for v2... so when this was announced I purchased on prelease and literally counted down the days. 

BUT NO SMOOTH TURNING OPTION!??   C'mon!   The VR version has smooth fwd,back, up and down but only has snap turning?   

I can't actually play it now until this feature is released.  Any update please?

reviews.review_title: Was hoping for Breathedge in VR
reviews.review_description: Really enjoyed the survival crafting game that was Breathedge, this game has elements of it and is a kinda fun 6 hour game with not much replayability. The game isn't bad, just a massive disappointment compared to Breathedge.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review! We are sorry that the game didn't meet your expectations, but we have always presented this VR game as a standalone experience within the Breathedge universe.

reviews.review_description: I may have to restart the whole game because of a glitch. I tried to put it in my  an item in my backpack, but it just disappeared. It would be nice if they added a restart mission.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback. We are sorry to hear that you encountered the problem. Let me clarify: after collecting resources, they are not reflected on the crafting station's screen?  Please write us an nikita.veselko@gmail.com with your problem and we will be sure to fix it as soon as possible!

reviews.review_description:
I initially gave this 5 stars but I keep encountering bugs that make it impossible to get through the beginning of the game. The bugs occur in relation to crafting the grappling hook thing to add to the multitool. Sometimes I’ll be able to craft and attach it yet a duplicate attachment still exists as if I hadn’t attached it yet. Other times I won’t be able to add materials to inventory that are needed for crafting (but I can add oxygen tanks). Each time, the game will not let me progress. 

I can’t find any way to reach the developers so I’m doing this edit to my review.

Original Review: 

Don’t be put off by the low star reviews, almost all are outdated complaints about how smooth turning wasn’t an option when game was first released. Devs added smooth turning after those reviews were made. It never bothered me as I believe the best way to play these types of games is to sit on a chair that spins all the way around. I don’t use the joystick in VR as often as some, it takes away from immersion.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! You can always email us at nikita.veselko@gmail.com with your questions and problems and we will help you solve them.

reviews.review_description: An absurd and exciting space adventure that will delight VR fans I would like to note the interaction using the multitool, the battle with the boss (you will rarely find such quality of implementation in VR games) and a fun quest with milking a cow
Played for half an hour, don't get others, who write about smooth rotation - really? I would feel sick already. Normal movement, if you ask me; and pulling yourself by hook is something.

reviews.review_title: Fantastic game! Hope they add content.
reviews.review_description:
25% off through here: bit. ly/cluckthecorperation (remove space)

I am a huge fan of the original game and for those who are also fans, here’s what you need to know.

Good:

This game is an insane amount of fun. It’s more of a combat and quest oriented game rather than a survival crafting game, but it’s still a very solid game. It has more of a cartoony jetsons feel to it than the original game, but given the graphics capability of even the quest 3, it works way better than trying to do a more realistic environment would. It also adds a lot of interesting backstory for the original game for those who were invested in the lore. 

Bad:

1) sadly, there aren’t really any funny game dev references like in the original, which in my opinion was the best part about breathedge. 

2) the non gamedev humor is definitely a little less crude than the original game. No making air tanks out of you know what. In addition, Man is sadly nowhere near as hilariously drain bamaged as in the original game. I’m not sure if they wanted to make it seem like something happened to him to make him so……… intellectually lacking…….. but tbh I’d rather him just be the same as in the original game as that would lead to a lot more funny moments.

3) this is not a huge experience. I’ve seen a few people complain that it’s a short game (3-6 hours on average) but the unfortunate thing about vr games is that’s about average for this price. It takes a lot more effort to make a good vr game and the amount of people buying a vr game isn’t very high so the price for vr games is a lot more per amount of content than regular games. I hope they will add more content in the future like they did for the original game.

Note for the developers:

I think a lot of the mechanics from the original game would translate very well to the vr game if you make a survival/sandbox mode Add-on. Like base building, research stations, vehicles, more advanced crafting, and looting. Not only would it add a LOT of replayability, but the quest store is sorely lacking a decent survival crafting game and the mechanics for most of that are either already developed for this game or could be implemented from the original breathedge using just lower poly models and lower quality textures. With the funny gags, fantastic graphics, and insanely fun movement and shooting mechanics, adding stuff like a survival crafting mode could make this one of the most popular games on the quest store.

I turn irl anyway so it doesn't bother me much, but strange to see no smooth turn option in a game all about movement. Love slinging around in space - so cool!
